topic.name: Regulatory Standards and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that establish standards and regulations for safety, education, environmental conservation, and financial practices.

Document 1: establish standards for determining the maximum passenger capacity for recreational vessels
require manufacturers and operators of passenger vessels to permanently display such maximum capacity
Document 2: awarding matching grants to enhance or improve state-funded preschool programs
require such programs to operate for at least a full academic year
Document 3: conduct and submit to Congress a full audit of hurricane protection funding
cost estimates associated with post-Katrina hurricane protection
Document 4: extend to all domestic corporations provisions allowing deferral of tax on gain from the sale of employer securities
establish the S Corporation Employee Ownership Assistance Office
Document 5: allow a new tax credit for 20% of rehabilitation expenditures for a historic home
increase the rehabilitation tax credit for residential rental property located in economically distressed areas
Document 6: Designates the North Palisade
in honor of the late David Brower
Document 7: establishes a Rare Cats and Canids Conservation Fund
provide assistance for the conservation of rare felids and rare canids
Document 8: Requires the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation to assess other federal regulations
determine whether the agency's proposal is in conflict with such regulations
Document 9: authorize services to be provided to individuals with Alzheimer's disease
who have not attained 60 years of age
Document 10: Waives certain restrictive Federal Power Act exemption qualifications
to generate power that does not exceed 1.5 megawatts

topic.name: Medicare Prescription Drug Policies
topic.description: Legislative amendments and policies related to prescription drug coverage and access under the Medicare program.

Document 1: Affordable Access to Prescription Medications Act of 2009 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act, the Public Health Service Act, the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), and the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to prescription drug plans, to limit the required co-payment or coinsurance for any one prescription to $200, and for all prescriptions in any month to $500.

Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), for plan years beginning on or after January 1, 2011, to expand the formulary tier exception request process to allow Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in a prescription drug plan to request an exception for a specialty prescription drug as a non-preferred prescription drug.
Document 4: Medicare Mental Health Prescription Drug Access Act of 2007 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover barbiturates and benzodiazepines as Medicare part D drugs, beginning in 2008.
Document 10: Part D Off-Label Prescription Parity Act - Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to permit a prescription drug plan (PDP) sponsor offering a PDP or a MedicareAdvantage (MA) organization offering an MA-PD plan to offer coverage of covered part D drugs for uses that are determined to be for medically accepted indications.

topic.name: Education Funding and Support
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational funding, support systems, and career readiness programs for students across different educational levels.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education of 1965 (ESEA) to authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states to integrate financial literacy education into public schools.
Prohibits states from using over 20% of their grant funds to train those teachers, develop curricula, and evaluate the impact of financial literacy education.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to include a space on the FAFSA form to identify students who are or were in the foster care system.
Notify students who identify themselves as such of their potential eligibility for financial aid under title IV and other federal programs.
Document 3: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to allocate and award funds to outlying areas under the State Fiscal Stabilization Fund program.
The State Fiscal Stabilization Fund program allocates and awards funds to states to support their funding of elementary, secondary, and postsecondary education.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require the Department of Education to award an additional Pell Grant to a student who has received a Pell Grant for an award year.
The total amount of the Pell Grants awarded to such a student for the award year may exceed the total maximum Pell Grant.
Document 5: Authorizes the Director of the National Academic and Career Innovation Center to award certain grants to state pathways partnerships to carry out activities that will create and enhance educational and career pathways for high school students.
Establishes the National Academic and Career Innovation Center to support the efforts of the state partnerships.
Document 6: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require local educational agencies receiving subgrants to describe how they will establish and carry out a school improvement program.
Involve institutions of higher education, employers, community-based organizations, parent organizations, and other stakeholders in the school improvement process.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an in-school facility innovation program contest to improve Indian school facilities for problem-based learning.

Amends part A (Indian Education) of title VII of the ESEA to establish a program awarding grants to local educational agencies to create or expand teacher and administrator pipelines for teachers of Native American students.
Document 8: Directs the Department of the Interior to establish an in-school facility innovation program contest to encourage institutions of higher education to solve the problem of how to improve Indian school facilities.
Amends the school improvement program to require states to develop standards-based assessments and classroom lessons that accommodate diverse learning styles.
Document 9: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to adjust the maximum annual and aggregate amount that a student may borrow under the Federal Perkins Loan program to account for inflation.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to partnerships for the development of national model after-school interdisciplinary education programs for secondary school students.
Gives grant and subgrant priority to programs targeting a high percentage of impoverished, struggling, or rural students.

topic.name: Temporary Tariff Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on the temporary suspension or reduction of duties on specific goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic synthetic staple fiber.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on 2-Propenoic acid, reaction products with o-cresol-epichlorohydrin-formaldehyde polymer.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on Propanoic acid, 3-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)-2-methyl, polymers.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain electric coffee makers with built in bean storage hoppers.
Document 5: provide temporarily for the duty-free treatment of certain women's textile upper footwear.
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Propanoic acid, 3-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)-2-methyl polymers.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on oysters (other than smoked), prepared or preserved.
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on certain nonwoven polypropylene zippered sleeping bag carry cases.
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of caprolactam disulfide with an elastomer binder.
Document 10: reduce temporarily the duty on certain sports footwear for men.

topic.name: Veterans' Health and Rehabilitation Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving health care, rehabilitation, and support services for veterans.

Document 1: program of individualized rehabilitation and reintegration plans for veterans with traumatic brain injury (TBI)
improving such veterans' behavioral and mental health functioning
Document 2: make available to veterans...specified information about educational institutions and the programs of education
additional requirements for institutions providing programs of education under VA and DOD educational assistance programs
Document 3: issuance and sale of a veterans health care stamp to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans
Document 4: permit the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to recover or collect reasonable charges for hospital care or medical services
An eligible veteran shall not pay a greater amount for receiving hospital care or medical services than the amount the veteran would pay for receiving the same or comparable care
Document 5: extends from 5 to 10 years after discharge or release from active duty after January 28, 2003, a combat veteran's eligibility for veterans' hospital care
Document 6: defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation
service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war
Document 7: reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses by veterans suffering the loss of hands, arms, or legs
additional veterans' disability compensation for service-disabled veterans determined by the Department of Veterans Affairs
Document 8: furnish to a veteran enrolled in the VA's patient enrollment system a drug or medicine that a duly licensed physician has determined such veteran is taking
ensure that all medications within the classes of antipsychotics and antidepressants...are available at all VA pharmacies
Document 9: ensure that veterans who are eligible for hospital care and medical services through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) have access to at least one full-service VA medical center
report to Congress on compliance with such requirement, including its effect on improving the quality and standards of veterans' care
Document 10: increases the amount of monthly subsistence allowance payable to veterans participating in the veterans' vocational rehabilitation program
pay such veteran a subsistence allowance for three months while the veteran is satisfactorily pursuing such program

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Exploration
topic.description: Legislation and policies governing the exploration, leasing, and production of oil and gas resources in offshore areas and the Arctic regions of the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain in Alaska.
Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a competitive oil and gas leasing program for oil and gas exploration, development, and production in the Coastal Plain.
Document 2: Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing and preleasing activities for any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS).
Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for oil and natural gas exploration and production.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act to redefine the term 'Alaska natural gas transportation project' to include in the natural gas pipeline system carrying Alaska natural gas to a market.

Confers upon the Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects responsibility for providing federal agencies with comprehensive information about Alaska natural gas transportation projects.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Visas
topic.description: These documents discuss various legislative acts related to immigration policies, visa categories, and the employment of nonimmigrant workers in the United States.

doc_1: H-2A nonimmigrant visa category for sheepherders or dairy workers
doc_2: require at least 350 miles of fencing along the southwest border
expedited removal of inadmissible arriving aliens
doc_3: repeal the denial of an unlawful alien's eligibility for higher education benefits
doc_4: increase employer civil fines for hiring unauthorized aliens
doc_5: establish a nonimmigrant visa category for professional nurses
doc_6: grant nonimmigrant visas to certain young minor children from Syria
doc_7: use the employment eligibility confirmation system to verify immigration status
doc_8: establish an H-2C nonimmigrant visa for agricultural labor
doc_9: include certain Haitian nationals for V-visa nonimmigrants
doc_10: authorize enlistment in the armed forces of aliens unlawfully present

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' research and development of electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to the designation, management, and enhancement of wilderness areas across the United States.

Document 1: releases the following areas in Colorado from further review for designation as wilderness
prohibits the inclusion of any portion of the Colorado River within the conservation area.
Document 2: designates specified federal land... as wilderness
sets forth provisions for the management of such land
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey... land within the Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest
to allow it to construct a ski-lift, gondola, or tramway
Document 5: designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park... as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
sets forth provisions for the administration of any land designated as wilderness
Document 8: amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands... to the Powderhorn Wilderness
designates specified lands... as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System

topic.name: Youth and Vulnerable Populations Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing abuse, trafficking, and violence against youth and vulnerable populations, including those in foster care, residential programs, and tribal communities.

Document 1: require the state plan for foster care and adoption assistance to demonstrate that the state has developed policies and procedures for identifying and screening children at risk of sex trafficking
documenting and reporting to law enforcement authorities of instances of sex trafficking
Document 2: require each location of a covered program to meet specified minimum standards
implement an ongoing review process for investigating and evaluating reports of child abuse and neglect
Document 4: encourage and assist in arrests and federal prosecutions for domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking in Indian country
develop multidisciplinary teams to combat domestic and sexual violence against Indians
Document 5: states should treat minor victims of sex trafficking as crime victims rather than as criminal defendants
demand for commercial sex with sex trafficking victims must be deterred through consistent law enforcement
Document 6: authorize local educational agencies to use program funds for peer support groups that provide an emotionally safe and confidential setting for youth
collect and publicly disseminate data on the incidence of dating violence, sexual violence, emotional abuse
Document 7: expand tribal criminal jurisdiction to include sex trafficking, sexual violence, stalking, and related conduct
Document 8: ensure that all programs funded under such Act include appropriate training in elder abuse prevention
develop a National Eldercare Locator Service
Document 9: includes as a homeless veteran a veteran or veteran's family fleeing domestic or dating violence, sexual assault, stalking
conditions in the current housing situation jeopardizing health and safety of children

topic.name: Mental Health and Support Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving mental health services, support systems, and treatment programs for various populations, including veterans, youth, and individuals with disabilities.

1: Helping HANDS for Autism Act of 2008 aims to improve services for individuals with autism spectrum disorders.
Requires grants for developing autism navigator programs to assist individuals with autism in accessing services.
2: Youth Mental Health Research Act establishes a network for youth mental health research and intervention services.
Focuses on early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people.
3: SERV Act authorizes grants to develop and enhance veteran's treatment courts for nonviolent offenders with mental health issues.
Programs include substance abuse and mental health treatment for veterans.
4: Stop Mental Health Stigma Act aims to reduce stigma associated with mental health conditions in specific communities.
Requires outreach and education strategies to promote behavioral health.
6: Mental Health First Aid Act provides training grants for mental health awareness and response.
Programs funded must educate on recognizing signs of mental illness.
7: Justice and Mental Health Collaboration Act expands assistance for veterans treatment court programs.
Focuses on collaboration among criminal justice, mental health, and substance abuse agencies.
9: Keeping Families Together Act aims to provide mental health treatment for children in state custody.
States must expand public health insurance for community-based mental health services.
10: Child Health Care Crisis Relief Act establishes programs for mental health services for children and adolescents.
Includes scholarships and grants for training mental health professionals.

topic.name: Health Policy and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This cluster covers various legislative acts related to health policy, drug regulation, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: Prohibits any funds appropriated for carrying out federal family planning programs from being made available to any family planning project if any service provider in the project knowingly provides contraceptive drugs or devices to a minor.
Requires providers to certify to the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) compliance with this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem a drug that is a sunscreen product whose labeling violates this Act to be adulterated.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to adopt a standard for broad-spectrum protection in sunscreen products.
Document 3: Establishes the National Commission on Federal Marijuana Policy to undertake a comprehensive review of current policies of the federal government toward marijuana.
Requires such review to include how federal policy should interact with state laws that make marijuana legal for such use.
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to award grants to states and nonprofit community organizations to distribute culturally sensitive educational materials about the dangers of opioid abuse.
Establishes a Controlled Substances Clinical Standards Commission to develop safe dosing standards for all forms of methadone.
Document 5: Establishes the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics within the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
Requires the Director to conduct activities to ensure the safety and effectiveness of FDA-approved drugs and licensed biological products.
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to add certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I.
A schedule I controlled substance is a drug, substance, or chemical that has a high potential for abuse.
Document 8: Ends the sale and distribution of pure hydrocodone bitartrate extended-release capsules within 45 days by withdrawing approval for its new drug application.
Prohibits the Commissioner of Food and Drugs from approving any new drug application for the capsules unless they are formulated to prevent abuse.
Document 10: Amends part D of the Social Security Act to revise requirements for the Medicare prescription drug program.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study and report to Congress on how to eliminate the gap in Medicare part D prescription drug coverage.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the management, restoration, and conservation of water resources and ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007 - Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality.
Deauthorizes certain projects authorized under the Water Resources Development Act of 2000.
Document 3: Bill Williams River Water Rights Settlement Act of 2014 - Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement.
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 4: Avra/Black Wash Reclamation and Riparian Restoration Project - Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Limits the federal share of the project's cost to 25%.
Document 6: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act - Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control.
Authorizes the Chief to construct demonstration projects to provide information to develop the study.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts to regulate mortgage practices, enhance access to home financing, and support affordable housing initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Truth in Lending Act to create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors
treat such a loan as a qualified mortgage
Document 2: require that credit risk retention regulations ensure no difference in treatment of asset-backed securities
securitized by the GSEs
Document 3: prohibits increases in Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac guarantee fees from being used in the Senate
to determine the budgetary impact for evaluating budget points of order
Document 4: establish a pilot program to encourage shared appreciation mortgage modifications
designed to return greater cash flow to investors
Document 5: authorize Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to purchase affordable workforce housing mortgages
subject to certain resale restrictions
Document 6: prohibits a court from entering a deficiency judgment requiring the mortgagor to pay any balance
after a foreclosure sale of the mortgaged property
Document 7: requires specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim
if the mortgage was not materially originated or underwritten in accordance with HUD requirements
Document 8: prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating or continuing a foreclosure proceeding
unless the mortgagee has determined whether the mortgagor is eligible for a qualified loan modification
Document 9: directs Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages
defined as existing first mortgages for purchase of, or refinancing another first mortgage
Document 10: authorize Ginnie Mae, Fannie Mae, and Freddie Mac to purchase affordable workforce housing mortgages
exempts such mortgages from any requirements and guidelines of such government sponsored entities

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at the protection, restoration, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
Authorizes funds for monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
Document 2: Expands the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds
NOAA must establish the Center of Excellence in Coral Reef Ecosystem Science Cooperative Institute.
Document 3: Extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
Gives priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 6: Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function
Modifies the goals and objectives of the national coral reef action strategy.
Document 7: NOAA and the Department of the Interior are authorized to undertake emergency actions
Establishes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 9: Extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
Modifies the Act's purposes, the goals and objectives of the national coral reef action strategy.
Document 10: Authorizes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.

topic.name: Agricultural and Food Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to agricultural practices, food assistance programs, and rural development initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to improve access to local foods by schools.
Incorporates experiential nutrition education involving school children.
Document 2: Extends funding for biobased markets and renewable energy system purchases.
Provides loan guarantees and grants to agricultural producers.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to make livestock indemnity payments for death losses.
Sets payment rates for livestock death losses due to adverse weather.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to designate California as a separate federal milk marketing order.
Document 5: Includes shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to reduce crop insurance assistance for converted native sod.
Document 7: Promotes the use of salad or fruit and vegetable bars in schools.
Provides grants for equipment needed to prepare food for school lunch programs.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to issue loan guarantees for school food service infrastructure.
Awards competitive matching grants for training school food service personnel.
Document 9: Revises the Small, Rural School Achievement program for rural local educational agencies.
Limits eligibility to LEAs serving a high proportion of children eligible for free or reduced price lunches.
Document 10: Eliminates the requirement for forage producers to participate in the insurance pilot program.

topic.name: Renewable Fuel and Clean Vehicles
topic.description: Legislation focused on the development, regulation, and incentives for renewable fuels and clean vehicle technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to qualify new flexible fuel hybrid motor vehicles for the alternative motor vehicle tax credit.
Defines 'new flexible fuel hybrid motor vehicle' as a qualified hybrid motor vehicle which is capable of operating on an alternative fuel.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to include the Secretaries of Agriculture and Energy in renewable fuel program activities.
Require a periodic review of adjustments in greenhouse gas reduction levels.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program, including by redefining 'renewable fuel'.
Revoking the renewable fuel standard for 2013-2022.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to approve the application of the state of California for a waiver of federal preemption of its motor vehicle emission standards.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to provide that a certificate of conformity issued by the Administrator of the EPA shall not expire.
Requires the Administrator to establish criteria for use in identifying similar vehicle makes, models, and emission standards.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Transportation to designate at least five Clean Vehicle Corridors along federal highways.
Defines 'cleaner alternative fuels' to include compressed natural gas, liquefied natural gas, and advanced biofuels.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a technology transfer center to disseminate information on research related to biofuels.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to implement a biofuels research, development, and demonstration program.
Document 8: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program to include 'cellulosic biofuel' within the definition of 'advanced biofuel'.
Fuel derived from algae among the types of fuels eligible for consideration as advanced biofuel.
Document 9: Establish a combined efficiency and renewable electricity standard that requires specified retail electricity suppliers to supply an increasing percentage of their demand from renewable energy.
Advancing or encouraging clean, renewable, alternative, innovative and/or efficient energy technology.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain businesses a general business tax credit for clean-fuel vehicle property.
Allows a new qualified hybrid motor vehicle tax credit for vehicles placed in service in nonattainment areas.

topic.name: Data Breach Notification and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the requirements for notifying individuals and authorities in the event of data breaches.

Document 1: Requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data.
Directs a covered entity that owns or licenses such data to give notice of any breach of the security of the system.
Document 2: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay any U.S. resident of a security breach.
Exempts agencies or business entities from security breach notification requirements if they provide written certification to the Secret Service.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information.
Document 4: Requires any federal agency or business entity engaged in interstate commerce that uses, accesses, transmits, stores, disposes of, or collects sensitive personally identifiable information to notify U.S. residents of a security breach.
Allows a delay of notification for law enforcement and national security purposes.
Document 5: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form.
Imposes a prison term of up to five years for willfully concealing a security breach that results in economic harm.
Document 6: Instructs the Director of Office of Management and Budget to establish policies for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security.
Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised.
Document 7: Requires any agency or person that owns or licenses computerized data containing sensitive personal information to develop reasonable security and notification procedures.
Permits a federal law enforcement agency to delay notification if it would impede an investigation.
Document 8: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act to expand requirements for reports on breaches of unsecured protected health information.
Directs the Secretary to issue regulations requiring covered entities to render protected health information unusable by unauthorized individuals.
Document 9: Amends provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act to prohibit the release of student educational records without written parental consent.
Defines 'authorized representative' for accessing student records.
Document 10: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable protected health information.
Makes any person who violates this Act liable to any person whose personally identifiable covered information has been disclosed.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Enforcement
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving tax enforcement, protecting taxpayers from fraud, and addressing issues related to identity theft and tax shelters.

Document 1: Identity Theft Improvement Act of 2010
eliminate the requirement in a prosecution for identity theft that the prosecution must prove that the defendant knew that the stolen identification documents belonged to another person.
Document 2: Stop Tax Haven Abuse Act
impose restrictions on foreign jurisdictions, financial institutions, or international transactions that are of primary money laundering concern or that impede U.S. tax enforcement.
Document 3: No Bonuses for Tax Delinquent IRS Employees Act of 2014
prohibits the payment of any performance award to an employee of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) who owes an outstanding federal tax debt.
Document 5: Senior Financial Empowerment Act of 2012
disseminate to seniors and their families and caregivers information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors.
Document 9: Taxpayer Protection Act of 2007
requires the Secretary of the Treasury to notify a taxpayer of any unauthorized use of such taxpayer's identity.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Stability
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial stability and regulatory oversight of banking and financial institutions in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision
Document 2: directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions
increase from 1.1 to 3.1 the debt-to-equity ratio allowable for a small bank holding company
Document 3: repeal certain discretionary exemptions of depository institutions from currency transaction reporting
require a depository institution to file a one-time notice of designation of exemption for each of its qualified customers
Document 4: requires the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to make the value of the U.S. dollar equal to price of gold
maintain and promote stable and effective financial markets
Document 5: amends the Commodity Exchange Act and the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
exempt from certain regulatory requirements any swaps and security-based swaps
Document 6: prohibits the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) from determining under the Financial Stability Act of 2010
requires FSOC to reevaluate annually its determinations regarding a nonbank financial company
Document 7: determining capital requirements or measuring an insured depository institution's capital
may treat a non-accrual loan as an accrual loan
Document 8: requires the risk-based assessment system to consider risks posed to the Deposit Insurance Fund
impose a systemic risk assessment on all systemically important depository institutions
Document 9: amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to require FSOC to consider the appropriateness of imposing such standards
FSOC must study every five years the impact of its determinations
Document 10: amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act and the Federal Credit Union Act
authorize the FDIC to impose special assessments upon depository institution holding companies

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation and Hunting Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing wildlife conservation, promoting recreational hunting and shooting, and managing related funding and programs.

Document 1: amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act
extend financial and technical assistance to the states for the promotion of hunting and recreational shooting.
Document 2: requires the Secretary of the Interior to report to Congress
expected increases in recreational hunting, fishing, shooting, and conservation activities.
Document 4: direct the Department of the Treasury to transfer, beginning in FY2016, revenues from energy and mineral development
to fund state wildlife conservation and restoration programs.

topic.name: Federal Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This cluster discusses the conveyance and management of federal lands and properties, including their use for public purposes and related regulatory considerations.

Document 1: convey, by quitclaim deed, to the town of Nantucket, Massachusetts, all interest of the United States in and to a parcel of real property
Makes the town responsible for all costs related to the conveyance.
Document 4: directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to offer to convey to the Galveston Historical Foundation the parcel of federal real property
Makes the Galveston Historical Foundation responsible for the costs of the appraisal and for all other costs related to such conveyance.
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to Summit County, Colorado, all interest of the United States in the approximately 40 acres of National Forest System land
Makes Summit County responsible for the processing and transaction costs related to the direct sale of the land.
Document 9: directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to convey, by quitclaim deed, to the National Women's History Museum, Inc.
Requires the purchase price for the property to be: its market value based on its highest and best use.

topic.name: Railroad Safety and Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at improving railroad safety, infrastructure investment, and operational efficiency in the rail industry.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2011 for the railroad safety program, including a safety risk reduction program.
Urges the development of new technology to prevent loss of life and injuries at highway-rail grade crossings.
Document 2: Sets forth Surface Transportation Board directives calling for effective competition among rail carriers and reliable rail transportation service for rail customers.

Document 9: Establishes the Presidential Commission on Women to review the status and federal aid to women.
Review data collection procedures regarding women federal initiatives.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop goals for clean energy technology deployment.
Requires annual reports to Congress on supported technologies and Corporation performance.

topic.name: Government Legislation on Education and Environment
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to education funding, resource management, and environmental conservation.

Document 1: Smart Grid Facilitation Act of 2007 supports modernization of electricity systems.
Instructs the Secretary of Energy to establish Smart Grid Investment Matching Grant Program.
Document 2: Establishes the Castner Range National Monument in Texas.
Interior shall manage the Monument to conserve and enhance its natural resources.
Document 3: Amends the Museum and Library Services Act to provide funds for open educational reading material courses.
Authorizes grants to facilitate the adoption of open educational reading materials.
Document 4: TEACH Act allows taxpayers a credit for tuition expenses for private or religious schools.
Document 5: Education, Achievement, and Opportunity Act provides a refundable tax credit for qualified education expenses.
Document 6: Western Hemisphere Drug Policy Commission reviews U.S. policies on illicit drug supply reduction.
Recommends a multi-year counternarcotics strategy for the Western Hemisphere.
Document 7: Establishes the Homeless Veterans Assistance Fund.
Allows taxpayers to designate overpayment of tax to the Fund.
Document 8: Nuclear Used Fuel Prize Act awards cash prizes for research on nuclear used fuel storage.
Document 9: Detroit Growth and Stability Act authorizes loans for city of Detroit.
Establishes City of Detroit Growth and Stability Fund.
Document 10: Sage-Grouse Protection and Conservation Act addresses management of sage-grouse species.
Requires monitoring and assessment of statewide conservation plans.

topic.name: Land Management and Use Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, withdrawal, and conveyance of federal lands for various purposes including military, recreational, and tribal use.

Document 1: Withdraws 5,100 acres of land near White Sands Missile Range in New Mexico.
Withdraws 37,600 acres of land near Fort Bliss in Texas and New Mexico.
Document 2: Establishes the Hurricane Sand Dunes National Recreation Area in Utah.
Provides for the conveyance of federal lands in Washington County, Utah.
Document 3: Allows the Miami Tribe of Oklahoma to lease, sell, convey, warrant, or transfer interests in real property.
Document 4: Withdraws approximately 512 acres of covered land in Arizona.
Grants the Secretary of the Interior exclusive jurisdiction to manage the Craigin Project.
Document 5: Requires amounts received by the United States from a lease under provisions relating to certain oil shale reserves to be deposited in the U.S. Treasury.
Requires that 50% of such amounts go to the Secretary of the Interior for environmental restoration.
